behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between modules: file loader + env overlay + schema/required-key validator + error formatter. A Unit test of only the validator could miss a real bug where env-to-config-path mapping is wrong and incorrectly reports a key as missing. Integration-brake check: no real network/subprocess required, no AI/ML output, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text is only about local config sources (file + env) and message content.
behaviors.test_description: Run startup config load with a temp config file missing a required nested key and with no corresponding `TOOL_...` env var; assert failure and exact missing path string (e.g., `database.host`) in the actionable error.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is module interaction: environment reader + path resolver + loader open path. Unit-testing only path-join logic can miss precedence/normalization bugs in startup wiring. Integration-brake check: no subprocess/network/real external API; correctness does not depend on AI output; no agentic sequence. Rejected System because behavior text only specifies local path resolution preference (`XDG_CONFIG_HOME` vs `~/.config`).
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a valid config at `tool/config.yaml`, ensure startup loads that file and not a separate file under `~/.config`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at merge precedence boundary across modules (YAML parse, env mapping, merge order). A Unit test of merge helper alone can miss bugs in env-name-to-key-path translation or load order in startup flow. Integration-brake check: no OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond local process state, no AI/ML output dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because behavior text is purely config precedence between local file and process env vars.
behaviors.test_description: Create config with a key value, set conflicting `TOOL_...` env var, run loader, and assert resulting effective config uses env value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction between YAML parser exception payload and CLI error formatting. A Unit test with mocked parser exceptions can pass while real parser line-number fields differ or are dropped by adapter code. Integration-brake check: no network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on parser/formatter behavior for a local file parse failure, not OS-level or external-process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML in a temp config file, run startup load, assert failure message includes a concrete line number matching parser-reported location.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local module wiring plus filesystem side effects (existence check -> mkdir -> default write -> continue load). A Unit test with mocked FS can hide path-join/order bugs and missing write calls under real temp-dir conditions. Integration-brake check: no subprocess/network/external API and no AI/ML dependency or agentic sequence. Rejected System because behavior text requires only local filesystem creation/write at startup, which is covered by integration tests using temp dirs.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp HOME/XDG location where config dir is absent; run loader; assert directory now exists, defaults file is created at expected path, and startup proceeds using that file.
